                                    WorkFlow
 This is how we usually use LLMs (Most
of time)




                                        3
 Why LLM Chatbot Is NOT Enough?
              "Can you schedule a meeting with ▪ Just a Chatbot
              my team next week and find a time  ‐ LLM cannot act or execute tasks
              when everyone is available?”       ‐ LLM cannot access personal or external data
                                                 ‐ You must provide all context manually
                                                 ‐ Output is often generic, not actionable
                    LLM does not know            ‐ Success depends more on quality of prompt
                    ▪   Your calendar
                    ▪   Your team members’ availability   ▪ Major Limitations
                    ▪   Time zone differences
                    ▪   Meeting preferences
                                                            ‐ No ability to access external tools (Perceiving
                                                              and Impacting External Environment)
                                                            ‐ No multi-step planning, reasoning or workflow
                                                              execution

Implication
▪ LLM cannot act or give you real answers
▪ You need to feed all the information as prompts

                                                                                                                4
 The Perception                                     -Action Gap of LLM
Chatbots
Scenario: A business analyst asks ChatGPT:
• "Analyze last quarter's sales data, identify declining product categories, and email the regional managers with a summary
  report."


Cannot Perceive
• The LLM has no access to the company's sales database.


Cannot Act
• It cannot send emails or trigger any external system.


Cannot Reason Over Multiple Steps
• It cannot plan a multi-step workflow.

This is why we need Agentic AI.
 What is Agentic AI
Agentic AI is an AI that can plan and take actions on its own to complete a goal, instead of just
answering a single question.
 Four Agentic AI Design Patterns
o   Reflection - Agent critiques its own output and iterates to improve quality


o   Tool Use - Agent calls external functions: APIs, databases, code execution


o   Planning - Agent decomposes complex goals into executable step-by-step plans


o   Multi-Agent Collaboration - Multiple specialized agents coordinate on complex tasks (Powerful and emergent)


Source: Andrew Ng, Agentic AI Design Patterns, DeepLearning.AI https://learn.deeplearning.ai/courses/agentic-ai/lesson/rm9bg7/agentic-design-patterns

Free Agentic AI Course!
 Pattern 1: Reflection
The agent evaluates and improves its own outputs through self-critique.

Process: Generate → Critique → Revise → Output
 Pattern 2: Tool Use
The agent calls external functions to interact with the real world:


o   Database Query: SQL queries to retrieve sales, inventory data
o   Code Execution: Run Python scripts for statistical analysis
o   Web Search: Search for market trends, competitor information
o   Email/Messaging: Send reports and notifications to stakeholders
o   File Operations: Read/write spreadsheets, generate PDF reports
o   API Calls: Connect to CRM, ERP, payment gateways
 Hands-on Activity 1: Tool Use in Agentic AI
  What You Will Build:
  An LLM-powered agent that can send emails using a custom tool, demonstrating the Tool Use design pattern.


  Technology Stack:
  Ollama: Run open-source LLMs locally
  LLaMA 3.1 (8B): Open-source LLM by Meta
  LangChain: Agent framework for Python
  SMTP (Gmail): Email sending protocol


  See the separate hands-on guide document for detailed step-by-step instructions.
  Python code Demo as following (We could schedule a time to work on a R code if you refer)
  https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1SJXmLdDkmhEzMYYfojekZB83vtyFslDl?usp=sharing
 Pattern 3: Planning
The agent breaks complex goals into executable sub-tasks.
Process: Receive Goal → Decompose → Execute → Monitor → Deliver
 Pattern 3: Planning




      Please Click Here for ReAct AI Agent Demo: ReAct Agent Flow
 Pattern 4: Multi-Agent Collaboration
Multiple specialized agents coordinate:
Data Analyst Agent: Queries databases, cleans data, performs analysis
ML Engineer Agent: Trains models, evaluates performance
Report Writer Agent: Compiles findings into business narratives
Coordinator Agent: Orchestrates workflow, ensures quality



Why Multi-Agent?
Each agent specializes, improving quality. Enables scalable automation of complex processes.

 Case Study: Multi-LLM Agentic AI for Retail
Analytics
RetailPulse Inc.
Mid-sized Australian retailer, 45 stores, ~$50M annual revenue

Business Problem
Manual forecasting takes 2 weeks, causes $2M in lost revenue from stockouts and $1.5M in excess inventory

Solution
Build a multi-agent AI system using multiple LLMs that collaboratively analyse sales data and generate managerial insights
Demonstrates ALL four agentic design patterns working together


Code link: https://colab.research.google.com/drive/1fl9t6JNY3R8W0oTRzHt9yIpFghjXgbIW?usp=sharing
 Case Study: RetailPulse Inc.
RetailPulse Inc.
Mid-sized Australian retailer, 45 stores, ~$50M annual revenue
Business Problem
Manual forecasting takes 2 weeks, causes $3.5M in losses
Solution
Two-agent AI system using open-source LLMs (Ollama + LangChain)
Agent 1: Data Analysis Agent - analyses data using tools
Agent 2: Business Insights Agent - generates prompt-driven managerial insights
Key Feature
Different prompts produce different insights for different roles
 Two-Agent Workflow Architecture
Data Analysis Agent (LLM 1 - llama3.1)
Uses Tool Use pattern. Tools: load_and_profile_data, engineer_features_and_train_models, compute_business_metrics,
create_visualisations. LLM decides which tools to call and in what order.
Business Insights Agent (LLM 2 - llama3.1)
Uses Planning pattern. Takes analysis results + user prompt. Generates tailored insights based on user's role.
Technology Stack
Ollama (local LLMs) + LangChain (agent framework) + scikit-learn
 Prompt-Driven Insights: Different
Roles, Different Answers
Same data, different insights depending on your prompt
Store Manager prompt: Gets staffing, inventory, and operational insights
Marketing Director prompt: Gets promotion ROI, campaign timing, seasonal strategy
CFO prompt: Gets cost savings, revenue impact, store performance analysis
Customer prompt: Gets best shopping times, deals, and product value insights

This demonstrates how agentic AI adapts to stakeholder needs
 Key Results from the Agentic AI
Workflow
Best Model
Gradient Boosting (R-squared = 97.22%, RMSE = $1,561). 3 models compared: Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Linear Regression.
Promotion & Holiday Lift
Promotion Lift: +8.6% sales increase with promotions. Holiday Lift: +42.0% sales increase during holidays.
Automation
Reduces 2-week manual process to under 1 hour
Open-source
Runs entirely on local Ollama LLMs (no API costs)
 Key Takeaways
o Traditional LLM chatbots cannot perceive or act in the external environment.


o Agentic AI extends LLMs with tool use, memory, planning, and reflection.


o Four design patterns provide a framework for building capable AI agents.


o Open-source LLMs with frameworks like LangChain make agentic AI accessible.
 Ethics & Governance AI (Next Week)
o Accountability: Who is responsible when an AI agent makes a wrong decision?


o Transparency: Agentic systems must log decisions and reasoning for audit trails.


o Human Oversight: Human-in-the-loop mechanisms for critical decisions.


o Data Privacy: Agents accessing databases must respect data governance policies.
                      Thank you for your attention and engagement!
